ranking.recommendation: RECOMMEND for Cluster C (Cardiff) — sole bidder with regional engineering presence and Tier IV plant. Recommend ALSO for Cluster A subject to TCO renegotiation (CapEx is 16% above field).
rationale_top3: Best-in-class technical solution (Tier IV, PUE 1.19, R1234ze GWP 7) with verified production deployments at HSBC London and Lloyds Manchester.
Only bidder with dedicated Glasgow + Cardiff night-shift teams — directly addresses category strategy R2 (skilled-engineer scarcity in Glasgow) and R3 (Welsh Water permitting).
Boilerplate accepted verbatim with zero contract deviations — closest to a clean award.
watch_flags: Trade press reports rumoured Stulz acquisition Q3 2026 — request M&A disclosure clause in contract.
Highest CapEx — challenge in negotiation; OpEx delta only partly closes the NPV gap.

ranking.recommendation: RECOMMEND for Cluster A (Slough East + Hams Hall + Reading West) — strongest UK delivery footprint in the M4 corridor where two of three Cluster A sites sit.
rationale_top3: Mature UK references at HSBC, NatWest, DWP at the same Tier III scale Centrica is buying.
SBTi 1.5°C validated and net zero 2040 — best evidenced sustainability trajectory of the two UK-strongest bidders.
Two boilerplate deviations (liability 100%, Net 45) are both negotiable on prior-deal precedent.
watch_flags: AI setpoint platform 'Aurora.IQ' is beta — exclude any innovation-tied service credit until first reference site reports 12 months production data.
Glasgow engineering bench thin (2 engineers) — do NOT award Cluster B.

ranking.recommendation: DO NOT RECOMMEND for award. Hold for future RFPs where their adiabatic engineering is the lead requirement.
rationale_top3: Adiabatic-Plus engineering is genuinely best-in-class for water stewardship (1.1 L/kWh) — would be Cluster C front-runner if not for SLA, F-Gas, and TPRM gaps.
5 contract deviations including F-Gas 2032 phase-down (breaches Centrica 2030 covenant) and PI insurance £15m vs £25m required.
Cardiff night-shift sub-contracted to unnamed Welsh partner — TPRM (Aisha Bello) cannot clear.
watch_flags: OpEx/CapEx ratio of 0.99 is anomalous — request supplier walkthrough of service-call frequency assumptions.
Send sorry-but-thanks with constructive feedback themes — this supplier should be invited back for water-led RFPs.
